summary: A rectangular, single-story walled compound with two tall corner towers and an interior grass courtyard. Exterior walls are striped horizontally with two light materials, topped by a brick parapet and stone coping. There are regular vertical white pilasters and an external low white block border around the perimeter. Two towers rise above the corners with overhanging flat cap roofs; small roofed entrances project from two sides into the courtyard.

shape.overall_form: rectangular outer wall forming an enclosed courtyard with interior open space and two corner towers
shape.symmetry: rough bilateral symmetry along the long axis (towers at opposite corners, matching wall bands and pilasters)

materials.name: quartz_block
materials.role: vertical pilasters and external low border

elements: two tall corner towers with overhanging flat caps
vertical white pilasters spaced along walls
interior grass courtyard with diagonal white markers/paths
low white block border around outer perimeter
small roofed entrance projections into courtyard
rebuild_hints: Construct a rectangular outer wall (approx 24x20 blocks) around a central grassy courtyard; walls are ~8 blocks tall with alternating horizontal stripes of two light sandstone-like materials separated by thin white trim.

Add regular white vertical pilasters (quartz-like) projecting 2-3 blocks out from the wall face spaced along the long sides; place a low white block border one block out from the wall base around the perimeter.
Top the wall with a 1-block high brick parapet with crenellated pattern and a stone/stonebrick coping around the very top edge.

Place two tall towers at opposite corners rising ~14 blocks high; towers use the same striped wall material but are more solid, with small square windows and an overhanging flat cap (stone coping, brick fill) forming a platform.
Inside the courtyard add diagonal rows of small white markers or slabs on grass (visible as diagonal pattern) and two small gabled/roofed entrance structures projecting from inner walls.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone vs smooth sandstone vs chiseled variants are visually similar; I labeled them generically as sandstone and sandstone_smooth with moderate confidence.
Height estimates for towers and wall are approximate (counted visually) and may be off by several blocks.
Interior details (below roofline and interior level) are not fully visible; placement and function of small interior structures are inferred from roof outlines.
Some trim blocks (coping and parapet edge) might be stonebrick or regular stone; confidence is medium for stonebrick.
